An announcement from Howkingtech International Holding Limited ( (HK:2440) ) is now available.

Howkingtech International Holding Limited announced that it has entered into five Subscription Agreements to issue a total of 49,049,824 new shares at a price of HK$1.714 per share. This issuance, which represents approximately 13.33% of the existing share capital, is under the General Mandate and does not require shareholder approval. The transaction is expected to raise approximately US$10.82 million. This strategic move could potentially strengthen the company’s financial position and market presence, although the completion of these subscriptions is subject to certain conditions.

The latest update is out from Huzhou Gas Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6661) ).

Huzhou Gas Co., Ltd. held an Extraordinary General Meeting (EGM) on November 14, 2025, where all proposed resolutions were unanimously approved. Key decisions included amendments to the Articles of Association, the appointment of Mr. Sun Xiaowei as a non-executive director, and adjustments to the company’s governance structure, such as abolishing the supervisory committee and modifying the composition of board committees. These changes are expected to streamline governance and enhance operational efficiency, potentially impacting the company’s strategic direction and stakeholder engagement.

More about Huzhou Gas Co., Ltd. Class H

Huzhou Gas Co., Ltd. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China, primarily operating in the gas industry. The latest update is out from China Longyuan Power Group ( (HK:0916) ).

China Longyuan Power Group has announced the establishment of three new subsidiaries, Rudong Longyuan, Yancheng Dafeng Longyuan, and Sheyang Longyuan, in collaboration with its subsidiary Hero Asia and Jiangsu Electric under China Energy. The transaction, valued at RMB153 million, will see these entities become subsidiaries of China Longyuan, enhancing its operational footprint in the renewable energy sector. This move is classified as a connected transaction under the Hong Kong Stock Exchange’s Listing Rules, requiring reporting and announcement but exempt from independent shareholder approval.

China Nonferrous Mining Corp. Ltd. ( (HK:1258) ) has provided an announcement.

China Nonferrous Mining Corporation Limited has announced a comprehensive rehabilitation project for the main and auxiliary shafts at its Chambishi Southeast Mine following an incident that resulted in an employee’s death. The rehabilitation, which involves significant structural repairs, is expected to conclude by mid-December, temporarily suspending production. Consequently, the company has adjusted its 2025 production guidance from 160,000 tonnes to 140,000 tonnes due to the temporary suspension of operations.

Ronshine China Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:3301) ) has provided an announcement.
